description:
Roll-up epic for the most strategic public-launch risk from the cold-context assessment (2026-06-22): the repo has no public answer to 'why mutagen_ex and not muzak?'.

The differentiator is real but buried in internal ADRs: a JSON-first, content-addressed, deterministic mutation tester explicitly designed so an LLM verifier/judge (not a human) consumes the output (.spec/decisions/no_pretty_output_v1.md, content_addressed_ids.md). Without surfacing this, the tool reads as a weaker muzak clone to a skeptical ElixirForum/HN audience.

Also: no empirical efficacy data is public, and the tool has never reported a mutation score on its own code (self_mutation_refused is architecturally necessary in-process).

This epic makes the value legible and credible. Independent of the other two epics; can proceed in parallel but should land before a public announcement.

## Children
- W1: README positioning section (why mutagen_ex, vs muzak/muzak_pro, LLM-oracle thesis)
- W2: real-world mutation score in README (run on a small real library)
- W3: dogfood — child-BEAM self-mutation score for mutagen_ex itself
- W4: mutation-testing literature grounding

comments.text:
ORCHESTRATED
Children closed: 4 (.1 positioning, .2 real-world score, .3 dogfood self-mutation, .4 literature grounding)
BOUNCEs encountered: 0 quality bounces (no AUDIT/VERIFY FAIL bounces)
  - .3 implementer respawned once (infra: Claude spend limit interrupted the first attempt before commit — not a quality bounce)
  - .3 verifier respawned once (malformed bw comment / cross-ticket /tmp contamination — posting failure, not a FAIL)
User escalations: 1 (Claude spend-limit decision: .3 retried on remembers profile, .2 additive README conflict resolved by orchestrator)

Epic branch epic/bqd (HEAD 7f2478b) carries all four children:
  0c35acc  .1  why mutagen_ex vs muzak positioning section
  22c29d2  .4  mutator catalog grounded in standard MT terminology (AOR/ROR/LCR/CRP + 6 Elixir-idiomatic)
  004f219  .2  real-codebase efficacy section (self-study 0.75; cross-project 0.67-0.75 band; representative survivors)
  7f2478b  .3  child-BEAM self-mutation dogfood: 46/79 = 0.58 aggregate, cited in README

All merge gates green (compile --warnings-as-errors, format --check-formatted, deps.unlock --check-unused; test 640/0). credo/sobelow N/A in this repo.

Epic left in_progress (phase:implementing) for the ship pipeline. The epic-branch → main PR is a deliberate human checkpoint — not pushed/opened by the orchestrator.

Follow-up candidates surfaced during the run (advisory, not blocking):
  - Pre-existing intermittent flaky test under random seed + --max-failures 1 (smoke). Not introduced by this epic.
  - .3: exact 0.58/46-of-79 figure reproduces but is pinned only by a >=0.5 floor; consider asserting the exact aggregate so README drift reddens CI.

DELIVERED. All 4 children closed; epic/bqd merged into main (merge 49f3635) and pushed to origin/main (a596705) with all gates green (compile --warnings-as-errors, format, deps.unlock, 640/0 tests). Docs/library epic — merge-to-main is the shipping evidence (no app deploy). A pre-existing cover_server test-isolation race surfaced by the merge was fixed in dba01be. Closing per /deliver.

title: [EPIC] hex.pm publish — why does this exist (positioning & efficacy story)
